qa_pairs.question: How is the number of core equivalents calculated for a job when a research group requests more cores than memory, assuming a core equivalent is defined as 1 core and 4GB of memory?
qa_pairs.answer: When a research group requests more cores than memory, exceeding the 1 core/4GB memory ratio, they will be charged by cores. For example, if a research group requests two cores and 2GB per core (for a total of 4GB of memory), this job request will be counted as 2 core equivalents when priority is calculated.
